zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard of Cornwall於1209年1月5日出生在英國英格蘭哈普爾維古姆時，他的父親John King of England42歲，母親Isabelle d'Angoulême Queen of England23歲。他於1231年3月30日與Isabel Marshal在英國巴金哈姆爾法利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1264年登記服兵役。他於1227年在英國波克夏被列為high sheriff of berkshire 。他去世於1272年4月2日在英國英格蘭賀福爾卡芙特 安布享年63歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾海利斯海利斯 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard of Cornwall于1209年1月5日出生在英国英格兰哈普爾維古姆时，他的父亲John King of England42岁，母亲Isabelle d'Angoulême Queen of England23岁。他于1231年3月30日与Isabel Marshal在英国巴金哈姆爾法利结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1264年登记服兵役。他于1227年在英国波克夏被列为high sheriff of berkshire 。他去世于1272年4月2日在英国英格兰賀福爾卡芙特 安布享年63岁，埋葬在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾海利斯海利斯 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Cornwall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from the county of Cornwall, which is named with the Old English tribal name Cornwealas. This is from Kernow (the term that the Cornish used to refer to themselves, a word of uncertain etymology, perhaps connected with a Celtic element meaning ‘horn, headland’), + Old English wealas ‘strangers, foreigners’, the term used by the Anglo-Saxons for British-speaking people.

variants.name: Cornwell
meanings: habitational name from Cornwell in Oxfordshire, named from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

variant of Cornwall or Cornhill; in southeastern England mainly a habitational name from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumberland) or one of the many minor places so called, often becoming Cornell .

variants.name: Corney
meanings: habitational name from Corney in Cumbria or Corney in Hertfordshire (now known as Corneybury), from Old English corn ‘grain’ or corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + ēg ‘island’. It seems possible, from the distribution of early forms, that it may also derive from a lost place in Lancashire. Alternatively, a habitational name from Corney (Lancashire), later named Corneyrow, now Corner Row or Cornoe in Greenhalgh (alias Greenall), in the parish of Kirkham. Corney was sometimes pronounced and spelled Cornah, Corner, Cornall, Gorney, and Gornall.
perhaps a shortened form of   Cornwall   or   Cornish  , or a metathesized form of   Croney  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard of Cornwall was born on 5 January 1209, in Winchester, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John King of England, was 42 and his mother, Isabelle d'Angoulême Queen of England, was 23. He married Isabel Marshal on 30 March 1231, in Fawley, Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He registered for military service in 1264. In 1227, at the age of 18, his occupation is listed as high sheriff of berkshire  in Berkshire, England. He died on 2 April 1272, in Croft Ambrey, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 63, and was buried in Hailes Abbey, Hailes, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard of Cornwall est né le 5 janvier 1209, à Winchester, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John King of England, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Isabelle d'Angoulême Queen of England, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Isabel Marshal le 30 mars 1231, à Fawley, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1264. En 1227, à l’âge de 18 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est high sheriff of berkshire  à Berkshire, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 2 avril 1272, à Croft Ambrey, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Hailes Abbey, Hailes, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard of Cornwall am 5. Januar 1209 in Winchester, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John King of England 42 und seine Mutter Isabelle d'Angoulême Queen of England 23. Er hat am 30. März 1231 in Fawley, Buckinghamshire, England Isabel Marshal geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er hat sich 1264 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1227, als er 18 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als high sheriff of berkshire  in Berkshire, England aufgeführt. Er starb am 2. April 1272 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Croft Ambrey, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Hailes Abbey, Hailes, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard of Cornwall è nato il il 5 gennaio 1209 nel Winchester, Hampshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John King of England, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Isabelle d'Angoulême Queen of England, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Isabel Marshal il 30 marzo 1231, nel Fawley, Buckinghamshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1264. Nel 1227, all'età di 18 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come high sheriff of berkshire  nel Berkshire, England. Morì il 2 aprile 1272, nel Croft Ambrey, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Hailes Abbey, Hailes, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard of Cornwallが1209年1月5日にイギリスイギリスハンプシュアイアウィンチェスターで生まれた時，父John King of Englandは42歳，母Isabelle d'Angoulême Queen of Englandは23歳であった。1231年3月30日にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアファウリーでIsabel Marshalと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1264年に兵役登録をした。1227年，18歳の時，イギリスバークシュアイアでの職業はhigh sheriff of berkshire と記録されている。彼は1272年4月2日にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアクロフト アンブリーで63歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアヘイレスヘイレス アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.San Silvestro, Orte, Viterbo, Lazio, Ita: 이탈리아 라지오 비터보 오트 산 실베스트로
ko.Fontrevault Abbey, Fontrevault, Maine-Et: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르
ko.Hailes Abbey, Hailes, Gloucestershire, E: 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 하일레스 하일레스 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Richard of Cornwall(이)가 1209년 1월 5일 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 윈체스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John King of England(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabelle d'Angoulême Queen of England(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1231년 3월 30일 영국 벅잉함실르 파리에서 Isabel Marshal(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1264년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1227년, 18세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국 버크실르에서 high sheriff of berkshire 이었다. 그는 1272년 4월 2일 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 크로프트 아브리에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 하일레스 하일레스 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard of Cornwall nasceu em 5 de janeiro de 1209, em Winchester, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John King of England, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Isabelle d'Angoulême Queen of England, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabel Marshal em 30 de março de 1231, em Fawley, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1264. Em 1227, com 18 anos, sua profissão aparece como high sheriff of berkshire  em Berkshire, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 2 de abril de 1272, em Croft Ambrey, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Hailes Abbey, Hailes, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard of Cornwall родился 5 января 1209 г. в местности винчестир, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John King of England было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Isabelle d'Angoulême Queen of England, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel Marshal 30 марта 1231 г. в местности фавли, буккингхамшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. В 1264 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1227 году, в возрасте 18 лет, работал по профессии high sheriff of berkshire  в местности биркшир, Англия. Он умер 2 апреля 1272 г. в местности крофт эмбри, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности хеилесь эбби, хеилесь, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard of Cornwall nació el 5 de enero de 1209, en Winchester, Hampshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John King of England, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Isabelle d'Angoulême Queen of England, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Isabel Marshal el 30 de marzo de 1231, en Fawley, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1264. En 1227, a la edad de 18 años, su ocupación registrada era high sheriff of berkshire  en Berkshire, Inglaterra. Murió el 2 de abril de 1272, en Croft Ambrey, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Hailes Abbey, Hailes, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
